summary: 2026-06-07 16:31:29 PDT - Reviewed `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, `git diff main...HEAD`, and `reviews/overlay-logging-quiet.md`. The branch is contained to the specified files and AC1/AC2 look aligned, but AC3 has a false-negative path in the anomaly guard. `npm test` could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest/Vite attempted to write `client/node_modules/.vite-temp`.

findings.title: Object-shaped payload breakages bypass raw anomaly logging
findings.claim: The new `overlay-message-raw` guard logs only when the normalized payload is falsy or `typeof payload !== 'object'`. That treats arrays and wrong object envelopes as usable snapshots. Since `normalizeIncomingMessage` returns parsed JSON arrays and object events without a `payload` key unchanged, a shape break like `[]` or `{ timestamp, data: ... }` would not emit `overlay-message-raw`, even though it is not a usable overlay snapshot. This violates the anomaly-only canary requirement by creating false negatives on real object-shaped breakages.
findings.suggestion: Use a stricter snapshot-shape predicate, for example non-null, non-array/plain object with the expected `status` key or minimum overlay-state keys, and add tests for a JSON array and an object envelope missing `payload` while keeping normal JSON-string and snapshot-object payloads silent.
